behavior: An almost continuously aerial insectivore; flies rapidly with stiff, shallow wingbeats and clings rather than perches when at rest.
field_marks: Very long, narrow, strongly curved wings
Short, tapered tail with stiff, bristle-like tips
Dark gray-brown overall, with a slightly paler throat
Tiny feet; clings to vertical surfaces rather than perching
Very short, broad-based bill that is often hard to see
habitat: Open airspace over a wide variety of terrain where flying insects are available; nests and roosts on enclosed vertical surfaces, including chimneys, cavities, caves, and similar structures.

sources.title: Chimney Swift Identification — All About Birds
